source.title: An information theoretical approach to prefrontal executive function.

confidence_rationale: Foundational theoretical paper (Trends Cogn Sci 2007) proposing an information-theoretical account of executive control in lateral PFC, with hierarchically-ordered control signals organised along the anterior-posterior axis. Posterior PFC handles sensorimotor mappings; rostral PFC handles increasingly abstract / branching / nested control. For SD-033e this provides the architectural framing in which the FPC analog sits at the rostral end of the executive-control gradient -- at the most abstract / branching / counterfactual level. Held below 0.8 because the framework is theoretical / synthesis rather than a specific empirical test, the gradient is one organising principle among several (Badre & Nee 2018 challenge / refine the strict A-P gradient), and the mapping from Koechlin's information-theoretical control levels to MECH-264/265 / SD-033e operating modes is by structural analogy.
failure_signatures: The strict A-P gradient framing has been challenged by subsequent work (Badre & Nee 2018) which argues for a more graded / integrated organisation. SD-033e should not lock to a strict A-P localisation as if it were decided.

The information-theoretical framework specifies hierarchically-ordered control signals but does not directly address counterfactual evaluation or competing-goal monitoring. MECH-264 and MECH-265 are specific architectural commitments that this paper supports only at the framework level.

The gradient is described as continuous; SD-033e's operating-mode commitment is discrete. The mapping requires the assumption that operating modes correspond to discrete bins of an underlying continuous gradient -- a workable simplification, not a derived prediction.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Lateral prefrontal cortex implements executive control via hierarchically-ordered control signals processed along the anterior-posterior axis. Posterior PFC handles sensorimotor mappings; rostral PFC handles increasingly abstract control levels (context, episode, branching). Executive control operates as a unitary function despite spanning multiple specialised regions because the control signals are integrated across the gradient. The framework is information-theoretical: each level handles a specific kind of contextual conditioning of action selection.
mapping.ree_translation: SD-033e Prong D commits to an FPC analog at the most rostral / abstract end of the executive-control architecture. Koechlin & Summerfield 2007 supports this architectural commitment by establishing the rostral-pole-as-most-abstract-control-level framing. Translated to REE: the FPC analog operates at a higher level of abstraction than SD-033a (lateral PFC analog, which handles rule-conditional bias) and SD-032 (operating mode) -- specifically, the FPC analog handles branching / counterfactual / competing-goal level control that lies above rule application. The implementation-level recommendation is that the FPC analog should not be wired in parallel with SD-033a but should sit hierarchically above it, with FPC outputs modulating SD-033a's rule_state when goal hierarchies shift.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The framework is theoretical and the strict A-P gradient has been challenged in subsequent work (Badre & Nee 2018). SD-033e should not lock to a strict gradient localisation. The mapping from Koechlin's information-theoretical control levels to MECH-264/265 / SD-033e operating modes is by structural analogy. The continuous-gradient framework is a partial overlap with SD-033e's discrete-mode commitment.
mapping.source_context: Trends Cogn Sci 2007 theoretical synthesis paper. Builds on Koechlin's earlier branching-and-cascade work (Koechlin 1999 Nature, Koechlin et al 2003 Science). Foundational anchor for the modern A-P-gradient view of lateral PFC executive control.

confidence_components.notes: Very high source quality (TiCS 2007, foundational A-P-gradient framework). Mapping fidelity moderate; the framework provides architectural framing but is one ARC removed from MECH-264/265 specific commitments. Transfer risk moderate; theoretical / synthesis vs primary data.
